Had high hopes… chips and salsa very good however my boyfriend ordered the fajitas and doesn’t like peppers so he said meat and onions only. I should have took a picture of the two whole onions they cut up and threw on the plate with a couple chunks of meat. We asked our server for a little more meat and she said it would be an extra charge. She said she’s afraid of the cook and didn’t want to ask. (Which is horrible and no one should be afraid of anything at work)
My order wasn’t anything to write home about either. Service was ok we had to ask two other servers for things and ours passed us 3 or 4 times without asking how things were. We went during weekday at a slow time.
Poor service, slow to attend to you and a waitress in a bad mood
Very slow service. The food was ok. I had better at outer places.
the waiter is super rude
Fajita was majorly vegetables!!

This place definitely used to be better. The food is average, I got a chicken burrito which had almost nothing in it but chicken. The rice portion was small and it didn’t come with beans (I should have double checked). They are kid friendly offering chicken tenders as a menu item for littles, and well as a juice assortment. They do have the best hot sauces!! Overall not the best Mexican, not even in the Plymouth area. They have very large margaritas which do have alcohol heavy pours. I got a strawberry “small” and my partner got the large mango, which was like the size of the Stanley Cup. So be prepared. We barely finished either.
Very loud, food was about the same as taco bell very disappointed

I believe this place is overrated!! Don't get me wrong, the people and restaurant were nice. Customer service was great. But the food is so bland! It is mediocre at best. I ordered a chicken and cheese quesadilla with a side of fries, and I was so disappointed. My boyfriend ordered steak quesadilla with veggies and chicken nachos (4/10).

Meal was awful  taco salad was loaded with grease from beef couldn't eat it
Drinks were very weak and charged different prices for the flavors of margaritas
Used to be my go to for Mexican food tried different locations food was awful  quality has gone way way down
Do NOT recommend this place any of locations  totally off my list of resturants  NOT worth money  had better food at Taco Bell  not even worth the ONE Star I rated

The staff was not accommodating my mother was choking it took them about 10 minutes to bring water we asked three times. I went to the car and got a bottle of water. And then they bought water. We had a large party of 20 people, and they would not split the bill. Last year, they did.

First, I have come to this location for the last 3 years for my daughter's birthday with 20-plus family members. The location had a lovely large room for my party. But once the host dropped off, the nacho thing went down. No water was placed at the tables. My 77-year-old mother in a wheelchair began choking and went and asked you to water nicely.  I was told someone would be over there in a few minutes.  I repeated my mother was choking. Can I have a glass of water?  You can hear her behind me. The host says someone will come. My wife left out and got some water from the car. Some food was average, and they sent a new waiter to help us and help him from a distance at the beginning.  We advised that several different families were coming. Can we have separate checks as we have the last several years, and he said yes. Once several orders are ordered, the waiter watching says it all needs one check. I felt they were not trying to serve us. I would have just left if I had not had family coming from a distance.  We will NEVER be back.

Got door dash from here.....  WHAT GARBAGE!!!!  No peppers, meat had to be cut up, put cheese on there when it was requested not to!!!!  Maybe I should call Trump!!!!!  OBVIOUSLY SOMEJUAN DOESN'T SPEAK ENGLISH!!!! SMH
Soggy, over priced for what you get, they charge you for chips they give for free it the restaurant, los tres in town way better!

Called in to get an order for pick up. A burrito and a fajita. Menu totaled up to $26.30, they were trying to charge me $44.50. Explained it was one burrito and one fajita. She said yes, $45.50. Well I canceled that order. I can see why their parking lot is always empty.

It's hot or miss on takeout, I personally will never eat or order there again.  We ordered inside for to go, the cook had to remake the shrimp chimichamga, it was all tied up and handed to us.  Get it home it's not a shrimp one it's chicken and barely any chicken in it.  Comments were made while waiting that the cook was drunk.  When I called to let them know the order was wrong, I was told it's posted it's our responsibility to make sure to go order is correct.  Well no, we were the only ones there, cook had to make it twice the bag tied tightly.  It's the servers job to make sure the order is correct.  There was no pico, lettuce sour cream.  I took the order back up, cause I can guarantee they would not have eaten it. I don't want my money back for the meal or the generous tip given. If the cook was drunk that does explain it of course your supervisor said he's fine.  I'll go with the other servers comments.  I've had food credits before from mi center location.  I can say for certain I will never be back.

I never have any trouble at any other location but this one. My order is wrong for carry out ABSOLUTELY EVERY TIME. No matter what. Something will be wrong, whether it's incomplete, not what I ordered, etc. It's gotten to the point I just assume it won't be right. I'm currently on a run of 4 carry out orders in a row being wrong in some way. And the last order I got I believe they are all of a sudden getting refried beans from a different supplier, because they used to be good and now they taste like cat food. I'm giving up for awhile. Maybe in 6 months they'll get my order right.
The sampler plate was terrible.  No beans, dry shredded chicken.  I would not recommend that to anyone.
Paid $46 for TWO Drinks,  ridiculous to say the least. Way over priced.

The food is not fresh. I ordered a burrito and the whole thing was ice cold, including the cheese sauce on top. Sent it back and they literally put it in the microwave and told me it was re-made. The tell tale sign was the burrito had a bunch of lettuce in it, which also got the microwave treatment. We had the $12 cheese dip for an appetizer, which made my wife and I violently ill later that night. The staff doesn’t care about your food or your experience, except for one waiter who was very polite. I will never eat here again, and will be warning all my family, friends, and colleagues not to eat here as well. The ownership should be ashamed of the state of this restaurant.

Went to Casa Tequila for an anniversary dinner and it was a mediocre experience. They sat us in the booth that is literally 2 feet from the host station and the seats felt like they had a metal rod laying just underneath the cushion.

The food was not very flavorful but the portions were adequate. I had the sampler plater and I will admit the pepper stuffed with cheese is pretty bussin. Otherwise, nothing special to write home about. Pricing was on the high side, no doubt to cover their rent for this prime slice of real estate.

Had lunch here with co workers today. Overall not a great experience. First red flag, no prices on the drink menu, second red flag, they do not have their gluten free menu on the website. If you have a gluten allergy you always want to check out their gluten free menu ahead of time. Third red flag, an employee laying on the sofa outside the ladies room. The burrito I ordered off the lunch menu was terrible! I literally spit it out. I don’t know if it was the sauce or some seasoning but it was awful. Also it came with refried black beans instead of pinto beans which was stated on the menu.The burrito also came with a slice of tomato. Everyone else had diced tomatoes. I asked for a chicken taco salad instead. It was completely devoid of any seasoning and my friend, the sliced tomato made a return on the taco salad. The 2 ladies that got the fajita taco salads enjoyed them. One of my lunch companions, who is gluten free, ordered the tostada and did not enjoy it. 2 people ordered large skinny margaritas and they were $23 a piece! I know 5 people who will not recommend or visit this establishment again. Oh and I was charged for the nasty burrito, not the taco salad

I had a terrible experience at Casa Tequila in Brighton and won’t be returning. The service was disorganized, and to make matters worse, I was overcharged on my bill. When I politely asked to see a receipt to understand the charges, the staff refused to provide one — which is not only shady but unacceptable.

There’s no reason a restaurant shouldn’t show you what you’re paying for. It felt like they were deliberately hiding something, and that’s a huge red flag. I left feeling ripped off and frustrated.

The food was average at best, certainly not worth the inflated price I ended up paying. There are plenty of other Mexican restaurants in the area that actually value their customers and operate with integrity. Avoid Casa Tequila unless you like surprise charges and zero transparency.
Waiter was terrible tired charging us for refills food was not good had to ask mutual times for a bill never actually seen the bill

The food was ok, the ambiance was ok. The seats were very shallow, and hard to sit on without slumping or turning sideways. And they used an exceptionally strong smelling cleaner on the floors, while we were eating, right by the table.  The scent was reminiscent of the cleaner they put on vomit in grade school.
Cool place food and service terrible. Never filled drinks, 7$ for a side of guacamole. Also kept my change when i paid cash… better Mexican restaurants out there for sure!

A group of my friends and I went to Casa Tequila yesterday on Cinco De Mayo. It was very busy I will say, however it took an hour and a half for us to receive our food! That is honestly absurd, I understand the kitchen being busy but that was just insane. Also, the food was not worth the wait at all.
Do not order the enchiladas. They are terrie. The beef is flavorless and relies too heavy on generic hot sauce for any flavor. Kids liked the chicken tenders and regular tacos though.

Didn’t even get the chance to eat here. Had a chance to meet the owner, impromptu he states that he owns a bunch of these because “he fills people up on rice and beans and charges a premium for it.” No thanks dude, what an awesome business model. This spot is for aging socialites and cat ladies. Avoid.
There better mexican places to go to
Tortilla soup does not contain rice or potatoes let alone both.
We ordered nachos bravos as a carry out and there was no cheese! How do you not put cheese on nachos. Very disappointed.
Went for my fathers birthday turned around and there was a cock roach coming out of the wall

Food is mediocre, service not great.Our server Brought the wrong food to half the table, no apologies or anything. Finally bring the correct meals long after everyone was done eating, the correct meal was Ice cold and full of giant fat chunks (carnitas), so sent that back out of frustration…again no apologies or offers to make it right.     The food that we did eat was just so-so, not planning to return here.

Third time I went to Casa tequila to have dinner at their food happy hour time 3 to 5. First time they Served us a nacho that was $13 but half off for the happy hour which Actually was a $13 nacho because it filled the plate. We had to take the rest of the food home that we Ordered because it was so much, the second time we decided to order the à la cart, which is also part of the happy hour half off meals. It was good tasting food every time but the last time we went, which will be my last time, we order the nachos again during happy hour, which is $13 half $6.50 and the plate was half of the plate. Then we ordered an à la cart happy hour half off burrito that was made totally different than the first time. I ordered it when they argued with me that I was wrong. I knew that this wasn’t a good place to ever come back to.
Someone needs to educate them on what half off means. It will not be me because I will not be back.
Cassa tequila a rip off $22 margaritas is insane.

Have eaten here twice and enjoyed food and service. Ate there July 3rd, food was good service for was unbelievable subpar. Had to repeatedly ask for drinks, food and bill. Ask twice to speak with a manager. Could not find anyone that understood what we were asking for. ??????

Went to the court house to get married and brought my wife and family for lunch and …… SUPER DISAPPOINTED!!!!! Literally waited over an hour order our food , we literally got full of the chips and salsa , finally ordered our food and when it was so salty and not cooked properly . I’ll never come back to this location ever again .
Just go to Garcia's on Lawrence & Western for  better and cheaper food and drinks. This place is overpriced & overhyped.
The space itself and staff are pleasant. However, the food just isn't good. It takes like its just out of the can. The burrito and enchilada were indistinguishable.
This was the most unpleasant experience for me. The tacos had flavor, but was mostly fat meat. I picked enough fat off 2 tacos to make 1. I will not be returning

After living on Division for over four years, my fiancé and I finally decided to give Casa Tequila a try — and regretted it almost immediately. From the moment we were seated, things went downhill. We were served stale chips with salsa that tasted like it came from a can — and not even a good one. I’ve had better salsa straight out of a grocery store jar.

My fiancé ordered a skinny margarita that somehow cost $20. Twenty dollars — for a basic drink. We ordered the standard fish tacos (bland), another kind of fish taco (also forgettable), and the quesabirria tacos (just okay). And for over $20 for three underwhelming tacos? Insane.

To try to justify the price, they serve the tacos on a ridiculously oversized 12-by-8-inch wooden plank that makes no sense. It looks more like a novelty cutting board than a plate. It’s clearly meant to create a false sense of presentation value, but when the tacos don’t even come with rice or beans, it just ends up being a giant slab of wood taking up 10% of your table for no reason.

Service didn’t help the experience. When I asked for the check, the server didn’t print one — instead, he brought over a handheld scanner and awkwardly held it up with pre-set tip options. I always tip 20%, and I did — but I’ve never felt more ripped off walking out of a restaurant.

This place clearly leans heavily on branding and aesthetics, because the food and value just aren’t there. I’ll be shocked if this establishment manages to stay open for another two years. There’s no way anyone who actually lives on this street would ever come back — especially not for overpriced takeout.

If you’re craving steak and a proper cocktail, go to Gibsons — you’ll spend less and enjoy it more. And if you’re in the mood for Mexican, just about any other spot within five miles will offer better food, better pricing, and a more authentic experience — without the gimmicks.

Worst experience I have had thus far called in to book my reservation a week in advance and they failed to add it to books basically said oh well it’ll be an hour and a half wait. Only thing they had to say was there is nothing we can do just pathetic Didn’t try to accommodate me for their mess up. Messed up my birthday dinner!!!!!

The drinks were good but my food was served with paper inside my enchiladas. When I showed the waiter he told me it was an onion. I even ripped the paper into pieces to show that it was paper. I will not being going back to this restaurant ever again. There was a previous time that I had dinner here and the food was served cold.

I think we need to stop with TikTok suggestions.
This place is overprice and overhyped for what it actually is. Food wasn't all that- their "specialty" steaks not really what you'd expect. I did leave the mashed potatoes because they were liquidy with veggies on top. Just not great. Drinks tbh I've had better and they are on the pricier side too.

Service our waiter in the beginning wasn't great but he actually turned it around and made it better by being friendly and talkative - so no problem there. They do take long on making drinks. Idk if they add alcohol or not on the margaritas but it was all sweetness. The busboys though man they need manners. They don't say excuse me when they drop the chips and salsa or when they pick up. They take everything and don't ask if we were done. That made me eh because we could still have been working on it and TIP (18%) is already included which I don't think is fair for 2 ppl.

Honestly- it could have been better.
Not worth it. Also was curious to who owns it and the strangest thing Ive seen: owners not disclosed lol. Wth? Not fair play bud, all restaurants and in general businesses disclose their owners

The drinks were the best part of the night

Tacos were not my favorite and or the sauce they came with

Food was under cooked. Servers were not very helpful. As I was paying I’m writing in the tip when I noticed a “ larger Party percent “ on the check. What constitutes a large party?? It was just me and my wife. Either way that “ larger party percent “ was 40 dollars. Definitely not going back.

Watch out for server OLIVIA. She likes to charge “large party fees” to small parties, after giving terrible service and being incredibly rude. She purposefully altered our receipt and tried to cross out the fee with sharpie. Super unprofessional and weird.
the server OLIVIA charged us for a large party fee and tried to get a tip out of us when there was only 4 of us. the server was rude and i hope she gets in trouble for everything she has done.
$15.50 per premade drink and then the waitress blocks out the automatic 20% tip trying to get another out of you.. Complete scam
Automatic gratuity fee and then they expect you to still tip

The manager was very rude. I came in to get drinks. They were clearly mad because I didn’t order any food. They were trying to get me to buy food. I only came for the drinks. They have a bar. He was rude didn’t even speak back to me. Raised the window for me to leave out and basically rushing me to finish my drink. I think it was because I was a black woman in his establishment. He treated the white woman that was next to me way different. I will never come here again do not recommend. They do not like black people. Only want your money.
Didn’t taste good at all

We weren’t aware of it being a automatic tip and not only that everything was charged individually when on the menu it doesn’t say that…it’s extra for cheese extra for beans it didn’t say that on the menu, on the menu it’s all together as one price and staff was very impatient as we were ordering as if he didn’t want us asking how anything was made

They don't tell you how long the wait might be for reservations. We ordered a Margarita and it was poured way below the rim of the glass. They said they were out of regular-sized glasses (the menu doesn't specify the size or that there's more than one glass option). If they don't have the right glass, they should fill the one they have or wash one of the correct size. We ordered queso fundido with chorizo ​​and the cheese looked like melted plastic. We asked for another tortilla and the waiter told us that if we wanted an order, he'd have to charge us an extra $4. Seriously, they're not willing to give us a free tortilla when we waited at least 30 minutes for a table, and then there was the glass incident???

Disappointing experience. I'm Mexican, and the taste and service are nowhere near what we offer in Mexico.

Save yourself the visit; it's not worth it at all.

Sub par Mexican food. All of the dishes lack seasoning. Meat is very bland. Ordered Margaritas.  They were nothing to remember. They were more of a sugary puree of fruit.
Parking can be difficult in the area. Residential permits are required on most  blocks or you can utilize paid metered street parking if you're lucky.
Nice service, great atmosphere for hanging out but it didn't meet my expectations or satisfy my taste buds. I would not return and can appreciate authentic Mexican food.  I suggest if you are looking for flavorful Mexican dishes seek another restaurant.

We visited during the weekend. It was our second time visiting The fajitas were delicious but the quesabirrias were just dry. And the drinks were awful. I asked for a margarita and it was so salty in top of that our waiter added the 20% gratuity when it was just me and my husband our bill came out to $250. Will not be visiting again.

I went Friday night an  hr before closing, we asking if the kitchen still open they said yes, The waiter Lucio, arrived at our table with a terrible attitude. He could have placed our drink order without even greeting us or giving us a smile. We greeted him and he never responded to our greeting. We ordered a wine and our food. We asked for mixed tortillas and he never told us that it was an extra charge. We also work in the restaurant industry and we understand that it was his last table, but we also understand that service and kindness come first. We gave him a very small tip because the truth is that he didn't deserve it! In the end he didn't even give us a thank you! Friend, if you don't like your job, maybe you're in the wrong place.

Food was okay. Drinks were weak. And server did some shady shit with the bill. He scratched the QR code out and the large party gratuity fee. Which was just me and my gf. I tip pretty gratefully but when a restaurant does shady shit like that. Never coming back.

Due to work schedules my girlfriend and I spent Christmas Eve at home and decided to order from this place … 100 dollars for 2 people I would expect my food with better presentation and much better tasting. My rice was covered in some red sauce as well as my beans. My chicken was tougher than leather and it was NOT GRILLED. My guacamole had huge chunks of onions and jalapeño and a huge piece of cucumber in the middle… I would have been happier with chipotle .

I called in an order. They told me it would take 10 minutes. I got there in 20 minutes and my burrito was NOT DONE.  They told me that I needed to wait for them to finish cooking it. What was the point of calling in if you don’t cook orders in advance!? On top of that, they charged me MORE for my order than what was on all of their menus and their online menu. The guy there said their prices changed and he would not honor the price on the website or in the menus sitting on their counters. They also messed up my very simple order— a steak burrito. They gave me chicken. The cheese sauce is really good, but that’s not enough to make me appreciate the poor service here. Not going to return.
THE FOOD WAS BLAND AND THE WAITER WAS VERRRY RUDE AND SARCASTIC! Definitely don’t recommend

Would not ever recommend this place! I end up leaving before I ordered. As soon as we sat down was told 20% will be added to our bill and it was only 2 of us. No one spoke English and they charged for additional salsa.

This place was anything but a good experience!!! Or table was rushed to order, eat, and leave!!! I will never go back,nor only because of the terrible service, but food quality wasn’t the best, so nothing is worth doing back!! Nope nope nope! 0/10- do not recommend!!!!!
The food was great but the service took forever and they said we could only be at our table for 90 minutes

Went yesterday and horrible experience,food was good don’t get me wrong but they literally gave me 4 piece of meat for 30 dollars in the molcajete. When it was time for the bill they added a 20 dollar tax and then tipped themselves 40 dollars for service which was a whole 60 dollars added. That could’ve have been 2 more meals. When asked why they said “oh people walk out the door without tipping” that sounded like a personal issue. I had brought along cash to tip but once seeing them tip themselves it was over. My older brother told him what if the service was wack, you can’t give yourself money when did this start because I was here last month. The man said they just started it 3 weeks ago🤦🏾‍♀️. As we finally just settled it and paid and was walking out, the man said to my brother “oh I’ll go sell drugs too” my brother don’t even sell drugs. And he stereotyped the hell out of him. Never again.

Went there expecting great food and service and was sorely disappointed! The staff was trying to clear our table while we were still eating. The waitress kept watching us as if we were gonna run out.  I had the chimichanga, the chicken was bland,  refried beans had way too much cheese.  We were a party of 4 and they added 20% gratuity for large party.  It gave feeling of assuming we wouldn't tip. Will never go back there again

Unfortunately, our visit fell far short of expectations due to multiple issues: The steak we ordered was not cooked to our specifications. Despite requesting medium well, what we received was significantly undercooked. We were served the wrong wine, which was not what we had ordered. There was no manager on duty to address our concerns or rectify the situation. Despite these issues, we empathize with our server, who appeared overwhelmed and was doing their best under the circumstances.Additionally, our order was incomplete as we did not receive our fries and asparagus. This experience was truly disappointing and does not reflect the level of service and quality one expects from casa tequila As a frequent diner, I have always valued the dining experience here, but this visit has left us reconsidering future visits.
I would appreciate a response regarding this matter and hope that steps will be taken to prevent such issues in the future.

unanswered.review_text: Today was my first time trying San Blas food in Lincoln Park, and after waiting an hour for the seafood tower, the waitress arrived with the bill and forgot about the tower. She brought my son two carne asada tacos instead of chicken, and we got four seafood empanadas and two Russian-style empanadas. The food was good, but the service was terrible. We spoke with the manager, and he gave us the food for free because of the misunderstanding, but honestly, I don't think we'll be back. I was very disappointed. We were the only people eating there, and they couldn't give us proper service.

Tonight at 7, my wife and daughter took me out to dinner at the Cedar St location, South Lansing. My wife drove, and they were taking me out for Mexican food. I was not driving. I asked to speak with the manager who insisted that they could not legally serve anyone without an actual picture ID showing their birth date.

I am 70 years old. I had my phone with digitized copies of my AARP card and my Medicaid card. I was told that none of that mattered. I have never been treated so poorly in a restaurant, and I have not been asked for picture ID when ordering alcohol since I was 25.

Has anyone else over 65 been carded here or anywhere else? ...and refused service because they were not driving, thus having no need to carry their wallet?

We decided to try our margaritas while we were waiting and one sip told us they were undrinkable, we love to drink and rarely complain about strong drinks, but these were straight tequila with a small dash of very bad sour. Our server seemed to be irritated with us due to all the issues and just walked away when I complemented the burrito (the only saving grace of the entire visit)
Not authentic. I sent my food back, it was gross. No cheese in a quesadilla? Nope.
Changed my review after visiting Los Tres Amigos on August 4, 2017. They have changed the menu, meals are much smaller, and it took an hour to get our meal. The wait staff was overwhelmed.
Service was awful

Went there yesterday 2 hours before they even closed. Our food was cold. I asked for no shrimp... my pineapple dish had shrimp in it. The whole thing was cold, my bfs burrito was cold, beans and rice were cold. It lacked flavor. It was our first time and we will never go there again. Taco Bell is better...
Over priced and slow service. and when I came back to take my order back the started yelling in the kitchen . Won't be going here anymore
Great food - very slow service on this particular day.  However, the abundance of locals tells me that it is worth it.
Food was horrible... only thing that was good was the chips and the tamale was tolerable.
The waiter was friendly but missing in action! Actually, it was the worst service that we've ever had here. Loved the food!

Food was wonderful, but the service was dreadful.  Several people that we attempted to order verde sauce from for our chips didn't have a clue what we were asking for, but smiled and nodded as they walked away.  Dispute over charges for drinks included in the kids meal was not handled at all gracefully.  The manager flat out asked if we were paying or not, while the girl working the register stated "well they used it they have to pay".  I have no problem paying, I just wanted to be charged what was agreed (as stated in their advertisement) before I placed my order.  The person checking out in front of me had a nearly identical encounter.  Obviously they do not care about us enjoying ourselves and providing good service.  I will never return here.

I had high expectations for this place from the reviews online. But when I got there it all went down the drain. To begin with the menu is the same few items presented in different ways and it WAS EXTREMELY OVERPRICED$$$$$$!!! Next my server didn't know the menu at all and it was obvious she didn't like half of the things I asked her. But when my food came out, which was extremely quick, it was hot..... That's mostly it the food was mediocre at best and isn't a real Mexican seafood experience. But I do give them credit for the fast and hot food and the free ceviche and tostadas. I don't know if I'll be back.

Honestly, not the best. Our waitress seemed really unenthused to be serving  us. The food itself was okay, but I did find a charred piece of meat in my vegatable fajitas. Which made me wonder what their kitchen must look like, hope its not riddled with cross contamination. You pay at the front of and I stood waiting for 5 of more mintues with another patron for someone to cash me out with no explanation as to why it took so long and no apology. The bathroom was also kind of gross. Overall the food was mediocre and the restaruant could have been more clean. I would not recommend this place. You'd probably get a better experience at the Taco Bell down the street to be honest.
Love the chips and salsa!!! Food is okay, kind of bland. :(

Unprofessional staff with no importance placed on the quality of food or customer experience. I will never do business with this restaurant again.

I ordered carry out and upon unpacking found that part of the meal was missing. Called the store and the woman who took the call immediately began making excuses and finally offered to let me come pick up the missing component. Screw it, I think I have some tortilla shells, I won't worry about it. Finish unpacking the meal only to find that the entree was not correct and the appetizer was tiny and of marginally acceptable quality. At this point I re-packed the food and drove back to the store.

I asked to speak to a manager and a man approached and asked what the issue was. After explaining the problems and ask for a refund, he disappeared and went searching for my order ticket (I thought he was processing a refund to my card). He came back to where I was waiting with the order ticket and pulled the entire order out, inspecting each item. He pointed out that the meal I had was the meal entered on the ticket, fetched the woman who took the order, and they proceeded to call me a liar. At this point I was beyond frustrated and began raising my voice demanding to be assisted by someone possessing a shred of competence. Finally a manager stepped in and, after asking too many repeated questions, gave me a refund.

What has happen to the service here? What once was outstanding, is now similar to an experience at fast food restaurant.

Chips and Salsa still hands down the best around. Everything else has fallen off tracks. It will be awhile before we return.

spent $25 for take out for mothers day, worst decision ever. the food was pathetic... nothing special about the food. small quantity and the food was horrible. they even forgot to give me my chimichanga

Horrible experience. They told us 15 minutes. After 50 minutes they gave us a number. Never called our name or number but claimed they did. They just gave our table away made no attempt to fix the situation. Ended up leaving.
Poor service
Everything was off!  Chips were old and tasted terrible! Salsa was below par.  Oder in dinning room. Food quantity was small.

I haven't been here since they redecorated, and it was shocking walking in to this disaster of decoration. The theme is so terribly overdone. There is no need to have every possible surface plastered with bright, distracting "Mexican" art. The food is standard tex-mex, not bad, not amazing.

I visited Los Tres Amigos located in Jackson MI last week and the restarant was gorgeous.  The food also was tasty and well prepared.

I did encounter a serious problem with the manager.  He brought our food to our table for our waitress who was very polite and wonderful server, with excellent people skills.  The manager gave me "Ricaro Morales as his name.  He was rude and uncourteous.  After eating I talked with him and he remained unapologetic.  I filmed our conversation.

I would be happy to talk to someone in management more about this.

Thank you for your kind considerationnin this matter.
Service is generally disappointing and the food is just okay.

Carryout. Order was ready 20 minutes later than they estimated. Food was burnt, over salted, and generally not worthy of delivery to the public. I don't mind waiting, or even having to wait a little longer if the food you've prepared is not up to standard. The meal I brought home to my family certainly was not worthy of even fast food standards. I'll give dine in one chance, but after other not so great experiences at other Los Tres restaurants, this will be the last chance.
My boyfriend and I stopped here to eat hoping it would be good, but have to say we will not be back to eat here food was horrible and not authentic we feel taco bell would of been much better to eat

The location on West Ave is terrible! Terrible service, terrible attitude, and terrible food! Coming from a Hispanic family, we use to stop in and grab a bite when I didn't feel like cooking. This last time was so gross I couldn't even eat it. Yuck! AND the manager is so rude there is no point in asking to speak with him. Save your money; tons of awesome places to eat in Jackson!

Well what can I say other than they need to communicate to their employees and their customers. I placed an order (130 dollars) for pickup on July 4th at 6:30 PM before my party. I was told on June 27, and July 1 that they WOULD be open on July 4th with normal hours (I called on July 1 just to be safe, because when I placed the order, no one seemed to know anything) and that I could pick it up, as I had payed in advance. Well, luckily I got there At 5 30, one hour before, to find they were CLOSED! I was absolutely livid. I have never had something like this happen. I gave them my phone number, name, and address, and I was not told that they would be closed even after confirming several times. This is honestly ridiculous and could've ruined my party. Luckily, Asia's Finest just down the road came in clutch and saved it. Never eating here again. I am expecting a full refund for food I NEVER RECEIVED! Make this right, management.
These guys double charged my credit card and acted like it was my fault.  They hung up on me twice.  Don't eat at any Los Tres Amigos they do not care about their customers.
Bad food bad service,  wont be back
Sad to say the place caught fire and will be out of service for sometime

Coming from a mexican family that loves to cook, I hereby declare the food served here pure garbage. The only thing here worth consuming is their huge margarita, that is if your wallet and liver can handle 40 dollars worth of tequila. The service is fast enough to decide that everything is slopped on a plate out of a vat of pork grease.
Food was over priced and very bland...won't be back

I had been going here for years, and the service has always been poor, something my friends and I overlook because we enjoy the food so much. Our most recent experience left us all speechless. We were overcharged, and the manager and server were the most rude service people I've ever encountered; not just in a restaurant, but anywhere. From the minute we walked in, we were treated in a hostile way by the server, who couldn't understand anything we were saying. As a result of this, we received things that we didn't order, and didn't receive things we actually ordered. When I approached the manager, he said that "there are other places I can go". He had no interest in accommodating us, and frankly, I feel like he was making up policies on the spot. Worst restaurant experience of my life. Never go to a Los Tres if you can help it.

trendAlerts.name: Mi Lindo San Blas Lincoln Park
